name: Methodology & Standard Hardening (External Anchor, Evidence Capture, Plan Amendment, Release Roles)

files_modify.purpose: Add two new sections (targeted additions only, no rewrite of Sections 1-7): (1) an **External Anchor Rule** stating that a consistency/QA check MUST anchor its truth reference on a source external to the artifact under test (e.g. the actual pipeline practice, a schema, a sibling protocol document, or run_state/evidence history) and MUST NEVER derive its expected value by grepping or re-deriving from the artifact-under-test's own content or phrasing -- cite the generic failure mode this remediates: a check that verifies a doc 'matches practice' by grepping the doc for its own claimed phrasing, which can pass even when the doc's claim is substantively wrong (the qa/002.json G6 finding: AGF's parse rule was checked against AGF's own wrong fragments). (2) a **Mandatory Adversarial Evidence** requirement: every QA round's Section 6 adversarial spot-check MUST be captured to its own evidence file (naming convention: <contract-id>-qa-adversarial.txt, e.g. .agent/evidence/NNN-qa-adversarial.txt), referenced by path from the QA verdict's adversarial_check object; a verdict whose adversarial_check has no corresponding evidence file on disk is incomplete per standard/anti_hallucination.md AH-3 and MUST be treated as a rejection, not a pass. Bump frontmatter `version` 0.1.0 -> 0.2.0 and append a `change_log` frontmatter entry (version 0.2.0, current date, one-line summary of both additions) per NDS Sec 4 / tools/validate.sh C8. Frontmatter stays schema-valid against schema/frontmatter.schema.json (all 6 required keys retained).

Add two new items (targeted additions to Section 3/4 area, no rewrite of Sections 1-5): (1) an **Evidence Capture Convention** specifying the exact shape every evidence file under .agent/evidence/ MUST take: the exact executed invocation as the FIRST line, the captured command output next, and a literal `EXIT:<code>` line as the LAST line recording the command's exit status; every evidence file MUST be replayable from the repo root (no assumed subshell cwd); and helper scripts or commands referenced inside an evidence file MUST be repo-relative or fully inlined -- session-absolute paths (e.g. a /tmp session-specific working directory, or any other path tied to a particular agent run's ephemeral environment) are FORBIDDEN inside evidence commands, since such paths are not reproducible by a later agent or human replaying the file from a fresh checkout. (2) a **Clock-Read Timestamps** rule: every durable-state timestamp (run_state.json history entries, contract/QA/evidence timestamps, change_log dates) MUST be read from the system clock at the moment of writing, never estimated, guessed, or reconstructed after the fact -- remediates the G4 finding (a planner self-reported estimate timestamp that was later found non-monotonic and had to be corrected with an annotation). Bump frontmatter `version` 0.1.0 -> 0.2.0 and append a `change_log` frontmatter entry (version 0.2.0, current date, one-line summary of both additions) per NDS Sec 4 / tools/validate.sh C8. Frontmatter stays schema-valid.

Add a new **Plan Amendment Rule** section (targeted addition, no rewrite of Sections 1-8): a post-phase or in-phase amendment that has been explicitly authorized by a human may be registered as a new or modified feature in feature_list.json by the orchestrator itself, PROVIDED the authorization cites a recorded authorization event already present in run_state.json's history log (an entry the orchestrator can point to, not a paraphrase); this is the orchestrator's own registration power and does not require routing through the Planner. By contrast, SUBSTANTIVE re-planning -- new scope that was not directly specified by the human's authorization (new architecture, new acceptance-test shape, anything the orchestrator would otherwise have to invent) -- MUST route through the Planner role, not be registered directly by the orchestrator. Scope-budget re-baselines (Section 6's cumulative-check halt, when a human authorizes raising original_estimate_lines/the ceiling) follow the identical pattern: human-authorized, orchestrator-recorded, and the re-baseline itself is recorded in the scope_budget object (a 'rebaseline' record naming from/to/authorized_by/at/reason), not as free-text history prose alone. Bump frontmatter `version` 0.1.0 -> 0.2.0 and append a `change_log` frontmatter entry (version 0.2.0, current date, one-line summary) per NDS Sec 4 / tools/validate.sh C8. Frontmatter stays schema-valid.

Add a new **Release Mechanics Ownership** section (targeted addition, no rewrite of Sections 1-6): the release-manager role -- typically the orchestrator, per standard/AGF.md's role set, since no separate release-manager role is defined -- owns three specific release-time mechanics once the Section 2 human sign-off gate is satisfied: (1) the changelog roll-up of CHANGELOG.md's [Unreleased] entries into the new version's dated section heading, (2) stamping that section with the release date, and (3) creating and pushing the annotated git tag. State explicitly that these three actions are release MECHANICS, not feature implementation -- they do not require a generator contract or the Loop 1/Loop 2 contract-first harness -- but they MUST still be performed on a dedicated branch (e.g. chore/release-vX.Y.Z) and merged via a reviewed PR like every other change in the repository, never committed directly to the mainline branch. Bump frontmatter `version` 0.1.0 -> 0.2.0 and append a `change_log` frontmatter entry (version 0.2.0, current date, one-line summary) per NDS Sec 4 / tools/validate.sh C8. Frontmatter stays schema-valid.

non_goals: No edits to methodology/01_execution.md or methodology/03_circuit_breaker.md -- both were already hardened in phase 001 feature 009
No edits to any file under standard/

No edit to root CHANGELOG.md -- feature 015 owns the phase-002 CHANGELOG entries; each edited doc's own change record lives in that doc's own change_log frontmatter, per tools/validate.sh C8's 'change_log entry OR CHANGELOG.md line' disjunction
No changes to tools/validate.sh, any schema/*.json file, or any validator/workflow logic -- this feature edits methodology prose and frontmatter only

No edit to methodology/README.md: AH-1 finding (verified 2026-07-08 by reading the file in full) -- methodology/README.md's table is a per-document PURPOSE summary, not a per-document VERSION table (no version numbers appear anywhere in the table or body); its own frontmatter version (0.2.0) and content are independent of the four docs' version bumps in this contract, so no drift is introduced and no edit is required to keep it consistent
No edit to any file beyond the four methodology/*.md files listed in scope.files_modify and this contract file

verification.acceptance_test: 02_adversarial_tdd.md contains an External Anchor Rule anchoring on the source of truth: grep -Eqi 'external anchor' methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md && grep -qi 'source of truth' methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md
verification.command: f=methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; grep -Eqi 'external anchor' "$f" && grep -qi 'source of truth' "$f" && echo PASS || { echo "missing 'external anchor' or 'source of truth' phrasing in $f"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: 02_adversarial_tdd.md mandates a captured adversarial-evidence file per QA round: grep -qi 'adversarial' methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md && grep -Eqi 'evidence file|evidence path' methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md -- SUPERSEDED (AH-1 pre-check finding): both clauses already match pre-existing, unrelated text in the current file (word 'adversarial' occurs 9x already; 'an evidence path' already appears at line 135 describing the existing adversarial_check object field, not a per-QA-round evidence-file mandate) -- a doc-wide grep would vacuously pass even without this contract's new requirement being added at all (the exact false-pass pattern methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md Sec 3.1 itself warns against). This command instead structurally locates the NEW heading (matching /mandatory adversarial evidence/i) as its own section, extracts only that section's body (up to the next heading of equal-or-higher level), and requires the section body itself to (a) reference 'evidence file' or 'evidence path', (b) name the per-round file-naming convention (a 'qa-adversarial' filename pattern, e.g. NNN-qa-adversarial.txt), and (c) tie an incomplete/missing evidence file to AH-3 / incompleteness -- anchoring the assertion on the new section's own content, not any doc-wide substring.
verification.command:
f=methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; python3 -c "
import re, sys
text = open('$f', encoding='utf-8').read()
lines = text.splitlines()
heading_idx = None
heading_level = None
for i, line in enumerate(lines):
    m = re.match(r'^(#{1,6})\s+.*mandatory adversarial evidence', line, re.I)
    if m:
        heading_idx = i
        heading_level = len(m.group(1))
        break
if heading_idx is None:
    print('no heading matching /mandatory adversarial evidence/i found')
    sys.exit(1)
end_idx = len(lines)
for j in range(heading_idx + 1, len(lines)):
    m2 = re.match(r'^(#{1,6})\s+', lines[j])
    if m2 and len(m2.group(1)) <= heading_level:
        end_idx = j
        break
section = '\n'.join(lines[heading_idx:end_idx])
checks = {
    'evidence file/path reference': bool(re.search(r'evidence file|evidence path', section, re.I)),
    'qa-adversarial filename pattern': bool(re.search(r'qa-adversarial', section, re.I)),
    'AH-3 / incompleteness tie-in': bool(re.search(r'AH-3|incomplete', section, re.I)),
}
failed = [k for k, v in checks.items() if not v]
if failed:
    print('Mandatory Adversarial Evidence section missing: ' + ', '.join(failed))
    sys.exit(1)
print('PASS')
"

verification.acceptance_test: 04_tool_driven_state.md defines the Evidence Capture Convention with the invocation-first / EXIT-last shape: grep -Eqi 'evidence capture convention' methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md && grep -q 'EXIT:' methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md
verification.command: f=methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; grep -Eqi 'evidence capture convention' "$f" && grep -q 'EXIT:' "$f" && echo PASS || { echo "missing 'Evidence Capture Convention' heading or literal 'EXIT:' marker in $f"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: 04_tool_driven_state.md forbids session-absolute paths and mandates clock-read timestamps: grep -Eqi 'session-absolute|/tmp' methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md && grep -Eqi 'clock' methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md
verification.command: f=methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; grep -Eqi 'session-absolute|/tmp' "$f" && grep -Eqi 'clock' "$f" && echo PASS || { echo "missing session-absolute/tmp prohibition or clock-read rule in $f"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: 00_planning.md defines the Plan Amendment Rule routing substantive re-planning to the planner: grep -Eqi 'plan amendment rule' methodology/00_planning.md && grep -qi 'authoriz' methodology/00_planning.md
verification.command:
f=methodology/00_planning.md; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; grep -Eqi 'plan amendment rule' "$f" || { echo "missing 'Plan Amendment Rule' heading in $f"; exit 1; }; python3 -c "
import re, sys
text = open('$f', encoding='utf-8').read()
lines = text.splitlines()
heading_idx = None
heading_level = None
for i, line in enumerate(lines):
    m = re.match(r'^(#{1,6})\s+.*plan amendment rule', line, re.I)
    if m:
        heading_idx = i
        heading_level = len(m.group(1))
        break
if heading_idx is None:
    print('no heading matching /plan amendment rule/i found')
    sys.exit(1)
end_idx = len(lines)
for j in range(heading_idx + 1, len(lines)):
    m2 = re.match(r'^(#{1,6})\s+', lines[j])
    if m2 and len(m2.group(1)) <= heading_level:
        end_idx = j
        break
section = '\n'.join(lines[heading_idx:end_idx])
# NOTE (AH-1 pre-check finding): a doc-wide grep for 'authoriz' would vacuously
# pass against pre-existing, unrelated Sec 6 scope-budget text ('explicit
# human authorization before any further feature is started') even without
# this section existing at all. Scope the assertion to this section's own
# body, and additionally require it names the orchestrator's registration
# power, cites run_state history as the authorization record, and routes
# substantive re-planning to the planner role.
checks = {
    'authoriz* within section': bool(re.search(r'authoriz', section, re.I)),
    'orchestrator registration power': bool(re.search(r'orchestrator', section, re.I)),
    'run_state/history authorization record': bool(re.search(r'run_state|history', section, re.I)),
    'planner routing for substantive re-planning': bool(re.search(r'planner', section, re.I)),
}
failed = [k for k, v in checks.items() if not v]
if failed:
    print('Plan Amendment Rule section missing: ' + ', '.join(failed))
    sys.exit(1)
print('PASS')
"

verification.acceptance_test: 05_release_train.md assigns changelog roll-up and tag mechanics to the release-manager/orchestrator role: grep -Eqi 'release[- ]manager|orchestrator' methodology/05_release_train.md && grep -qi 'roll-up' methodology/05_release_train.md
verification.command: f=methodology/05_release_train.md; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; grep -Eqi 'release[- ]manager|orchestrator' "$f" && grep -qi 'roll-up' "$f" && echo PASS || { echo "missing release-manager/orchestrator role assignment or 'roll-up' phrasing in $f"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: Combines and supersedes two feature-014 acceptance tests: (7) "Each of the four edited methodology docs bumped version above 0.1.0: for f in 00_planning 02_adversarial_tdd 04_tool_driven_state 05_release_train; do grep -Eq 'version: 0\.[1-9]|version: [1-9]' methodology/$f.md || exit 1; done" and (8) "Each version bump has a change record: for each edited doc a change_log entry in its own frontmatter OR a root CHANGELOG.md line naming it (validate.sh NDS Sec 4 check passes)". SUPERSEDED per the 011 lesson (feature 011's original substring grep 'version: \"1.1.0\"' was replaced with a parsed YAML assertion): this command parses each doc's own frontmatter block with PyYAML (not substring grep) and asserts, per doc, that `version == '0.2.0'` exactly AND that `change_log` contains an entry whose `version` field equals `'0.2.0'` exactly -- structurally verifying both the bump and its change record together, anchored on the parsed frontmatter object rather than a raw-text pattern match.

Supersedes feature-014 acceptance test (9): "All four edited docs still carry the 6 required frontmatter keys and remain schema-valid: bash tools/validate.sh; test $? -eq 0". This is the dogfood anchor: tools/validate.sh's C8 check will independently FAIL if any of the four version bumps lacks its change_log record (verification entry 7 above only checks the record exists in isolation; this entry proves the full repo-wide validator -- all 8 checks, not just C8 -- accepts the edited tree end to end). Strengthened beyond the literal `test $? -eq 0` to additionally assert the exact summary line, so a validator that silently skipped or miscounted a check cannot false-pass this entry.
verification.command: test -f tools/validate.sh || { echo "MISSING tools/validate.sh"; exit 1; }; out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out"; echo "$out" | grep -qx 'SUMMARY: 8 passed, 0 failed' || { echo "expected exact summary line 'SUMMARY: 8 passed, 0 failed', got: $(echo "$out" | grep '^SUMMARY:')"; exit 1; }; test "$rc" -eq 0 && echo PASS || { echo "tools/validate.sh exited $rc, expected 0"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (not in feature_list_002.json; a negative/out-of-scope-edit-detector check per methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md Sec 5, dogfooded on this very contract): scope guard confirming the non-.agent working-tree diff touches exactly the 4 contracted files, no more and no fewer -- catching an accidental leftover edit or missed file (e.g. methodology/README.md, CHANGELOG.md, standard/*, or 01_execution.md/03_circuit_breaker.md, all explicitly out of scope per this contract's non_goals).
verification.command: changed=$(git diff --name-only HEAD -- . ':(exclude).agent' | LC_ALL=C sort); expected=$(printf '%s\n' methodology/00_planning.md methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md methodology/05_release_train.md | LC_ALL=C sort); if [ "$changed" = "$expected" ]; then echo PASS; else echo "scope drift -- got:"; echo "$changed"; echo "expected:"; echo "$expected"; exit 1; fi

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/014-verify-NN.txt, where NN is the 1-based index of the command in the 'verification' array, zero-padded to 2 digits, in array order (e.g. .agent/evidence/014-verify-01.txt for the first entry, ..., .agent/evidence/014-verify-09.txt for the ninth). Each evidence file follows methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md's Evidence Capture Convention (exact invocation as the first line, captured output next, literal EXIT:<code> as the last line). One file per command; no inline proof output in this contract or in run_state.json.
